Interface PixelWriter

All Known Implementing Classes:
IntBufferPixelAccessor
public interface PixelWriter
Allows modifying pixel data of an image/framebuffer in ARGB format.
See Also:
  • ARGB
  • OffscreenFramebuffer.createPixelWriter()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default void
    fillARGB(int x, int y, int w, int h, int argb)
    Fills a rectangular region with the given color.
    int
    getHeight()
    Returns the height of underlying image data.
    int
    getWidth()
    Returns the width of underlying image data.
    void
    setARGB(int x, int y, int argb)
    Sets a single pixel.
    default void
    writePixels(int[] src, int off, int scanSize, int startX, int startY, int w, int h)
    Copies pixel data from an array.

  • Method Details

    • getWidth

      int getWidth()
      Returns the width of underlying image data.
      Returns:
      the width, always positive

    • getHeight

      int getHeight()
      Returns the height of underlying image data.
      Returns:
      the height, always positive

    • setARGB

      void setARGB(int x, int y, int argb)
      Sets a single pixel.
      Parameters:
      x - the X coordinate
      y - the Y coordinate
      argb - the ARGB color
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if x or y are out of bounds

    • fillARGB

      default void fillARGB(int x, int y, int w, int h, int argb)
      Fills a rectangular region with the given color.
      Parameters:
      x - the X of top-left corner
      y - the Y of top-left corner
      w - rectangle width
      h - rectangle height
      argb - the ARGB color
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if part of the region is out of bounds

    • writePixels

      default void writePixels(int[] src, int off, int scanSize, int startX, int startY, int w, int h)
      Copies pixel data from an array.

      A pixel at (x, y) is read from the given source array as: 

      src[off + (y - startY) * scanSize + (x - startX)]
      (row-major order).
      Parameters:
      src - the source array (ARGB)
      off - data offset in the source array
      scanSize - number of pixels per row
      startX - the starting X coordinate
      startY - the starting Y coordinate
      w - width of region to copy
      h - height of region to copy
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if part of the region is out of bounds
